data.title: Walmart facing slowing comparable store sales—Cleveland Research
data.description:
• Walmart (WMT) shares are trading defensively on Wednesday as a bearish note from Cleveland Research is being circulated across Wall Street.
  • The firm warns that Walmart's (WMT) U.S. comparable sales “appear to have slowed to some degree” with a potential risk to consensus estimates “depending on how July plays out.” 
  • The company appears to be working to reduce inventory through price adjustments, using tariff refunds to offset lower prices.
  • “The tone on the outlook sounds slightly less positive based on macro/consumer concerns,” Cleveland Research writes, with these pressures driving slower comparable store sales.
  • With shares down more than 5%, Walmart (WMT) is in negative territory for a sixth consecutive day and on track for its lowest close in 8 months. This is Walmart’s (WMT) longest losing streak since last October.

data.title: Gold shares see a ‘dark cross,’ worst three months in over a decade
data.description:
Gold shares (GLD) had the worst three months in over a decade, Renaissance Macro Research analysts pointed out.

Gold (GLD) showed a “dark cross” for the first time this cycle, confirming a “bubble indicator” from last year’s fourth quarter, according to the analysis.

A dark cross is a bearish chart pattern that occurs when an asset’s 50-day moving average crosses below its long-term moving average. In this case, its 200-day moving average. This pattern suggests that short-term selling pressure is taking over, and a prolonged period of decline could follow.

 

 

 

“It was the worst 3 months in over a decade,” analysts said.

The catalysts include “a hawkish Warsh,” a firm dollar (DXY), and steady ETF outflows: “Arithmetic, a non-yielding safe -haven, can't win,” Renaissance Macro said.

Gold shares (GLD) are down about 5.3% year-to-date, down more than 8% from a month ago, and from three months ago, GLD is also down more than 8%.

Gold and Gold Miner ETFs: (GLD), (IAU), (SGOL), (OUNZ), (BAR), (GDX), (GDXJ), (NUGT), (RING), and (DUST).

data.title: Warsh sees Fed boosting use of real-time data, reducing reliance on government data
data.description:
Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh said he expects the review of the economic data the central bank uses in formulating monetary policy will help the Fed achieve its objectives of maximum employment and price stability.

Last month, he announced the  formation of five task forces to review the central bank's practices, including one focusing on data.

"My hope, my aspiration, is that nine or 12 months from now, we're going to be using new technologies to understand what's happening in the real economy in a contemporaneous, real-time way that positions us as central bankers to make better decisions," Warsh said during a panel discussion at the European Central Bank Forum on Central Banking.

He hopes that "we're no longer going to have to rely solely on data we get from government agencies with mismeasurement problems that have surveys that are no longer relevant." . 

Warsh made the statements in response to a question of what his favorite data point is. "My favorite data is upon us," he said. "And if we do our jobs, we'll be here a year from now, and we'll say we discovered data that helps us make better decisions. And we live up to our promises, we strengthen our credibility, and politics stays at bay."

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Vertiv opens new plant in Malaysia to meet AI data center power needs
data.description:
Vertiv (VRT) said Wednesday it opened a manufacturing facility in Malaysia, expanding its manufacturing footprint to support growing demand for AI and high-density computing infrastructure across Asia.

The facility in Johor—Vertiv's (VRT) first in Southeast Asia—will make power and cooling systems needed to keep clients' data centers running in Southeast Asia, North Asia, Australia, and New Zealand, and is expected to become fully operational in 2027, the company said.

"Asia continues to be one of the fastest-growing regions for AI and digital infrastructure investment," Vertiv (VRT) CEO Giordano Albertazzi said. "Expanding our manufacturing footprint in Malaysia aims to further enhance our ability to support customers with quality, speed, scale, and resilience."

data.title: Abu Dhabi-based AI investment firm MGX raises $49B for its Fund I
data.description:
The Abu Dhabi-based artificial intelligence investment firm MGX has raised $49B for its Fund I, which is $4B more than it expected, which will be used to support its stakes in AI-focused companies such as semiconductors, infrastructure and large language model developers.

So far, MGX has invested in 14 AI or AI-related companies and has partnered in global projects with Microsoft (MSFT) and BlackRock (BLK). Some of the companies in MGX's portfolio include Anthropic (ANTHRO), OpenAI (OPENAI), Databricks, TikTok (BDNCE), SpaceX (SPCX), and Vantage Data Centers North America.

"The firm launched Fund I to provide highly curated exposure to the AI thematic through disciplined investments in differentiated and restricted opportunities across the AI technology stack, including semiconductors, AI infrastructure and AI and AI-enabling technologies and platforms," MGX said in a release.

Fund I attracted investment from institutional and private investors from the Gulf, North America, Asia and Europe. MGX is chaired by Sheikh Tahnoon bin Zayed Al Nahyan, the national security advisor for the United Arab Emirates, and backed by G42 and Mubadala Investment.

data.title: Don't be fooled by the headline: Tariff refund inflates Nike's FQ4 EPS, margin
data.description:
For the fiscal fourth quarter ended May 31, Nike (NKE) collected $300 million in cash tied to claims for tariffs imposed under the IEEPA, with the remaining benefit recognized as accounts receivable while the company awaits reimbursement.

“In the fourth quarter, we determined that the financial recovery of claims related to incremental tariffs paid under IEEPA had become probable. This resulted in the recognition of a one-time benefit of $986M, offsetting the IEEPA tariffs embedded within the cost of sales,” Nike CFO Matthew Friend said during the company’s earnings call.

The company also included 900 basis points of tariff recovery in its gross margin, without which GM would have been compressed by 10 basis points to 40.2%.

While Nike (NKE) felt the adjustment was appropriate, the outsized benefit from tariff refunds did not go unnoticed by analysts, who viewed the inclusion as a distortion of Nike’s (NKE) results.

The inclusion of tariff refunds (both earned and unearned) “flattered an otherwise broadly in-line print,” admits RBC Capital Markets analyst Piral Dadhania, while Seeking Alpha analyst Luca Socci agrees that the tariff refund put lipstick on an otherwise underwhelming quarter. 

“Nike reported a top and bottom-line beat. However, we have to take this with a grain of salt because EPS came in at $0.72 thanks to a $0.52 benefit from tariff refunds. Without this one-off event, the gross margin would then have been 40.2%, which, by Nike’s standards, is low. So, the beat is not truly real, and investors should not be fooled by the headline,” Socci adds.

More crucially, as tariff recovery benefits begin to wear off, Nike (NKE) still expects tariff-related pressure on profits.

“The tariff environment continues to be uncertain,” Friend added on the call. “Our forecast is based on incremental tariff rates of 10% continuing through the end of July [when Section 122 expires] and then increasing to 15% thereafter.” This assumption means Nike (NKE) expects earnings to be flat in Q2.

So, while the tariff refund was a welcome boost, it is not a sustainable one. The next phase of Nike's turnaround will have to be driven by stronger fundamentals rather than one-off gains.

data.title: Saab signs $2.5B fighter jet deal with Ukraine
data.description:
Saab (SAABF) (SAABY) said late Tuesday it signed a contract to deliver 16 Gripen E fighter ​aircraft to Ukraine in a deal worth ​~24.6B Swedish crowns ($2.54B), part of an agreement previously announced by Sweden’s Prime Minister Kristersson and Ukraine's President Zelensky.

The two leaders met last year and signed an air defense cooperation agreement that included the potential to export 100-150 Gripen jets to Ukraine; as part of the agreement, Saab (SAABF) (SAABY) said it signed the ~$2.5B deal to provide 16 new jets in addition to spare parts, related equipment, and technical support, with deliveries scheduled in 2029-30.

Sweden will also donate an additional 16 older Gripens from its current fleet, with first deliveries expected early next year.

Saab (SAABF) (SAABY) has now added ~$7.4B of new orders to its backlog recently, after it outlined a deal Monday to deliver three submarines with a training and support package to Poland.

data.title: Circle Internet rated Neutral at Susquehanna, Compass Point after OPEN-USD news
data.description:
Susquehanna initiated coverage of Circle Internet Group (CRCL) with a Neutral rating and $69.00 price target, while 

Shares were +0.73% at $63.09 during pre-market trading on Wednesday.

"A first mover in a strategic market, we believe CRCL is building one of the most important franchises in digital finance. From '26-'28 we forecast robust ~29% revenue and 33% EBITDA CAGR," said analyst James Friedman.

According to the trading firm, bull cases are stablecoin's USDC (USDC-USD) estimated use cases and the token replacing legacy settlement systems moving on-chain and revenue diversification beyond interest-rate-sensitive reserves.

Meanwhile, the bear cases are the company's mostly reserve income, shared economics, and the stock trading at full valuation.

Susquehanna's rating aligns with Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating of Hold. The Wall Street analysts as well as SA authors grade the stock as Buy.

Separately, Compass Point raised its recommendation on CRCL to Neutral from Sell and reduced its price target on the stock to $55 (-$42) after the announcement on Open USD (OPEN-USD) stablecoin.

Compass Point views the announcement as bearish for CRCL's long-term opportunity in payments.

However, the research firm doesn't expect OPEN-USD to gain traction within DeFi or crypto trading, which remain USDC's core use cases today.

"Given USDC's limited traction within payments, we don't believe OUSD's success impacts CRCL's EBITDA trajectory over the next 1-2yrs," said analysts Ed Engel and Michael Donovan.

"In the near-term, we still acknowledge downside risk if Clarity Act dies in the Senate next month. However, we also expect CRCL and COIN to renew their USDC partnership on 8/18, which could reduce an overhang on CRCL's valuation," said the analysts.

"We see fewer headline risks until big banks launch a deposit token network in H1'27," said the research note.

data.title: S&P 500, Nasdaq decline at the start of the second half of the year as the tech trade struggles
data.description:
Wall Street’s major indexes were mostly lower on Wednesday, with the Nasdaq Composite ((COMP:IND)) struggling to stay above water due to technology stocks declining amid ongoing volatility.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last -0.1%, while the heavy-tech Nasdaq (COMP:IND) was -0.4%, and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) was +0.2%.

Among the worst decliners within tech were Micron (MU) -6.3%, Sandisk (SNDK) -8%, Nvidia (NVDA) -2.2%, and Broadcom (AVGO) -1.5%.

Meta Platforms (META), on the other hand, jumped more than 8.7% after Bloomberg reported it is building a cloud business for excess artificial intelligence compute.

On the economic side, the ISM Manufacturing PMI fell more than expected in June, while PMI Manufacturing unexpectedly dropped.

In addition, construction spending ticked up in May, slightly lower than expected, and the U.S. private sector added 98,000 jobs in June, missing the +117,000 consensus, according to ADP.

Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh said on Wednesday that the Fed will “chart a new course” on forward guidance, contrasting with his predecessor's practice of indicating which way policymakers were leaning in upcoming meetings.

Bond yields were mostly muted. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was 1 basis point lower at 4.46%, while the 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) dropped 5 basis points to 4.15%.

Crude oil futures (CL1:COM) were lower at $68, while Brent (CO1:COM) was $71 per barrel.

data.title: AI infrastructure, chip stocks fall after report Meta building cloud business for excess AI compute
data.description:
Chip and AI infrastructure-related stocks fell on Wednesday after a report that Meta Platforms (META) is planning for a cloud infrastructure business to sell access to AI computing power and models.

Meanwhile, Wall Street averages were mixed. The tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) fell around 0.33%. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) dipped about 0.03%. Meanwhile, the blue-chip Dow (DJI) rose about 0.22%.

Shares of Meta jumped about 8% following the report. Meanwhile, cloud computing leaders Amazon (AMZN), Microsoft (MSFT), and Alphabet (GOOG) (GOOGL) were largely in the green.

However, AI infrastructure solutions providers Nebius (NBIS) slumped nearly 12%, CoreWeave (CRWV) plummeted about 10%, and Super Micro Computer (SMCI) fell around 4% on Wednesday.

Shares of AI chip giant Nvidia (NVDA) fell about 2%. On Tuesday, the United States Office of Government Ethics released President Donald Trump's 2025 certified financial disclosure, which showed that during 2025, Trump owned  at least $5M in Nvidia, at least $1M in Palantir shares, and at least $5M in Apple. Meanwhile, Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) fell nearly 3%. Broadcom (AVGO) declined around 2%, while Qualcomm (QCOM) and Cerebras (CBRS) were largely flat. However, the second quarter saw big tech stocks gaining, with AMD surging 185.56%.

American memory giant Micron Technology (MU) slumped about 7% despite announcing it secured a long-term Strategic Customer Agreement with General Motors to supply the automaker with memory and storage platforms necessary for vehicle production. In the second quarter of 2026, Micron surged about 241%.

Meanwhile, Sandisk (SNDK) tumbled about 8% even as Bank of America upped its price target to $2,500 from $2,100, citing the growing supply-demand imbalance. Seagate Technology (STX) fell around 4%. Western Digital (WDC) declined nearly 5%.

Other AI and networking-related stocks were also largely in the red on Wednesday. Corning (GLW) plummeted about 10%, while Lumentum (LITE) slumped nearly 4%. Celestica (CLS), Coherent (COHR), and Ciena (CIEN) each fell around 3%, while Arista Networks (ANET) declined about 2%. Cisco (CSCO) and Applied Optoelectronics (AAOI) each dipped about 1%.

Shares of Intel (INTC), Arm (ARM), Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM), and GlobalFoundries (GFS) each tumbled about 4%, while Marvell Technology (MRVL) fell nearly 3%. Lattice Semiconductor (LSCC) declined about 2%, while Analog Devices (ADI) was largely flat but in the red. However, Texas Instruments (TXN) was in the green.

Chip equipment makers: KLA (KLAC) tumbled about 8%, while Lam Research (LRCX) slumped nearly 7%. Applied Materials (AMAT) fell around 6%, while ASML (ASML) declined nearly 4% on Wednesday.
